pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and install_ugene.sh download NM_000207.3 via eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov, defaulting to it before falling back to J00265.1

privileged_info_summary: The task downloads and operates on real biological data from NCBI GenBank. However, there is a critical biological discrepancy: setup_task.sh downloads NM_000207.3 (human insulin mRNA transcript) by default, instead of the genomic DNA J00265.1 which contains the promoter region. Because it searches mRNA instead of genomic DNA, promoter elements like the TATA box are not present (TATA_box expected count is 0). The fallback ground truth in verifier.py (TATA=1, E_box=5, GC_box=2) is completely fabricated and matches neither the mRNA nor the genomic DNA sequence exactly. Fortunately, the setup_task.sh script dynamically generates ground truth based on the actually downloaded file, yielding expected motif counts of TATA_box:0, GC_box:1, polyA_signal:1, E_box:6, and CArG_box:0 for the NM_000207.3 sequence.
